articles.title: Dollar Rallies as Inflation Risks Push Bond Yields Higher
articles.expand: The dollar index (DXY00 ) rallied to a 2.5-week high on Friday and finished up by +0.47%. The dollar rallied on Friday on concerns that strong US economic news and soaring crude oil prices will prompt the Fed to tighten monetary policy, a bullish factor for the dollar. articles.title: Week Ahead: Rising US Rates Underpin Greenback
articles.expand: The US economy appears to be re-accelerating here in Q2 after nearly grinding to a halt in Q1 (0.5% annualized pace). April US CPI and PPI were more elevated than expected. The anticipated average effective Fed funds rate in December rose more than 15 bp in the past week and is up slightly more than 75 bp since the war on Iran began. The Dollar Ind...

articles.title: US Stocks end lower on mounting inflation worries
articles.expand: U.S. stocks declined Friday as rising crude prices fueled global inflation fears, pushing Treasury yields higher and making equities less attractive. The Trump-Xi summit yielded few results, while Jerome Powell's tenure as Fed chair concluded amidst concerns about sticky inflation and potential rate hikes. articles.expand: India’s reserve position with the IMF also edged up by $12 million to $4.875 billion, the central bank data showed.

articles.title: Democrats accuse Trump of stock trade corruption
articles.expand: Democrats accused US President Donald Trump of corruption on Friday after the disclosure of major stock market transactions carried out in his name, although his son denied there was wrongdoing.

articles.title: AutoCanada Q1 Earnings Call Highlights
articles.expand: AutoCanada (TSE:ACQ) reported first-quarter 2026 adjusted EBITDA from continuing operations of CAD 31 million, down from CAD 43 million a year earlier, as the company continued to work through a soft Canadian automotive market and margin pressure in used vehicles. articles.expand: European markets experienced a significant downturn, with the STOXX 600 index sliding due to escalating energy inflation fears stemming from the U.S.-Iran tensions. Rising costs are impacting consumer and producer prices, prompting expectations of interest rate hikes. Economically sensitive sectors and defence stocks bore the brunt of the sell-off,...

articles.title: After Rs 3 fuel hike, can petrol and diesel prices rise further? Experts decode
articles.expand: After petrol and diesel prices were raised by around Rs 3 per litre across metros, concerns are growing over whether consumers could face another round of fuel hikes. Analysts say continued under-recoveries, elevated crude oil prices and government decisions could determine the next move.

articles.expand: Australia has “wasted billions” making housing unaffordable through investor tax breaks, but a radical budget shake-up changes everything.

articles.title: Finance & Forex Weekly Recap: May 11 – 15, 2026
articles.expand: Hotter-than-expected CPI, a blowout PPI, a new Fed chair, and a Beijing summit long on ceremony but short on breakthroughs — for the dollar, a very good week.

articles.title: Forecasting the upcoming week: Markets shift focus to PMIs and central bank events
articles.expand: The US Dollar Index (DXY) climbs above the 99.30 region, reaching fresh multi-week highs on Friday as stronger-than-expected United States (US) economic data reinforced expectations that the Federal Reserve (Fed) may keep interest rates elevated for longer.

articles.expand: Powell exits amid another bond market surge Jerome Powell is leaving the Fed chairmanship at a time when the bond market is once again under pressure. The 10-year Treasury yield is posting its largest weekly rise since April 2025, climbing 23.5 basis points, or 5.39%, in just one week. After ending 2025 near 4.16%, the yield fell to a low of 3.926%...

articles.expand: The price of silver is under intense pressure today, tumbling $6.55 or -7.84% to $76.90. articles.title: Euro: Growth downgrades and softer outlook – Societe Generale
articles.expand: Societe Generale economists highlights that Eurozone 2026 GDP forecasts have been cut more than United States (US) projections in percentage terms, reinforcing United States (US) outperformance.

articles.title: Euro falls as hawkish Fed expectations boost the US Dollar, Treasury yields
articles.expand: The Euro (EUR) extends losses against the US Dollar (USD) on Friday, with EUR/USD slipping to near one-month lows as hawkish Federal Reserve (Fed) expectations boost the Greenback and US Treasury yields.

articles.title: Japan: Solid GDP but JPY still weighed by trade – DBS
articles.expand: DBS economists Taimur Baig and Radhika Rao expect Japan’s 1Q Gross Domestic Product (GDP) to grow 1.8% QoQ saar, supported by firm exports and AI- and semiconductor-related investment, keeping their 0.5% full-year GDP forecast on track.

articles.title: Canada: Energy lifts CPI, BoC focus on core – TD Securities
articles.expand: TD Securities’ Senior Canada Economist Robert Both expects Canadian Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation to rise to 3.1% year-on-year in April, driven mainly by higher energy and food prices and base effects from last year’s carbon tax changes.

articles.expand: The U.S. inflation shock is lifting Greenback demand, but Canada’s oil linked economy is pushing back. At some point, one of these forces has to blink.

articles.title: UK GDP Q1 2026: Economy Grows But the Best Is Already Behind It
articles.expand: The UK economy grew 0.6% in Q1 2026, matching forecasts and marking its fastest growth in a year — but analysts warn the best may already be behind it.
